lede: A look at July 8 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Walter Scheel and Nelson Rockefeller, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Shinzo Abe
events.desc: Former Japanese prime minister Shinzo Abe is shot and killed with an improvised firearm due to resentment against the Unification Church.

events.title: Israel
events.desc: Israel launches an offensive on Gaza amid rising tensions following the kidnapping and murder of three Israeli teenagers.

events.title: Sudan Airways Flight 139
events.desc: Sudan Airways Flight 139 crashes near Port Sudan Airport during an emergency landing attempt, killing 116 of the 117 people on board.

events.title: Germany national football team
events.desc: West Germany win the FIFA World Cup final against defending champions Argentina, with Andreas Brehme scoring the game's only goal.

events.title: Aeroflot Flight 4225
events.desc: Aeroflot Flight 4225 crashes near Almaty International Airport in the Kazakh Soviet Socialist Republic (present day Kazakhstan), killing all 166 people on board.

events.title: Canadian Pacific Air Lines Flight 21
events.desc: Canadian Pacific Air Lines Flight 21 is destroyed by a bomb near 100 Mile House, Canada, killing 52.

events.title: Unidentified flying object
events.desc: Reports are broadcast that a UFO crash-landed in Roswell, New Mexico in what became known as the Roswell UFO incident.

events.title: Rugby union
events.desc: The first rugby union test match between the Wallabies of Australia and the Springboks of South Africa is played at Newlands Stadium in Cape Town.

events.title: St. John's, Newfoundland and Labrador
events.desc: St. John's, Newfoundland is devastated in the Great Fire of 1892.

events.title: Vasco da Gama
events.desc: Vasco da Gama sets sail on the first direct European voyage to India.

births.name: Walter Scheel
births.line: German soldier and politician, 4th President of West Germany

births.name: Nelson Rockefeller
births.line: American businessman and politician, 41st Vice President of the United States

deaths.text: Kim Il Sung, North Korean commander and politician, President of North Korea
Jean Moulin, French soldier and leader of the French Resistance
Oscar I of Sweden, King of Sweden and Norway from 1844 to 1859
Christiaan Huygens, Dutch mathematician, astronomer, and physicist
Albert of Saxony (philosopher), Bishop of Halberstadt and German philosopher (born circa 1320)

observances.text: Christian Feast Day: Saints Peter and Fevronia Day (Russian Orthodox)
